title: asperula aristata var. pubescens
intro: Asperula aristata var. pubescens, also known as Wood Sorrel or Wood Geranium, is a perennial herb belonging to the gentian family, characterized by its small white flowers and hairy leaves. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and inflammation, due to its astringent and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Asperula aristata var. pubescens include its ability to soothe digestive problems, such as diarrhea and ulcers, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Asperula aristata var. pubescens can be found in tinctures, infusions, and salves, which are often used topically to treat skin irritations and wounds.
